methods.name: ClosestMeshPoint
methods.signature: public MeshPoint ClosestMeshPoint(Point3d testPoint, double maximumDistance)
methods.summary: Gets the point on the mesh that is closest to a given test point. Similar to the ClosestPoint function except this returns a MeshPoint class which includes extra information beyond just the location of the closest point.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: The source of the search.

params.name: maximumDistance
params.doc: Optional upper bound on the distance from test point to the mesh. If you are only interested in finding a point Q on the mesh when testPoint.DistanceTo(Q) < maximumDistance, then set maximumDistance to that value. This parameter is ignored if you pass 0.0 for a maximumDistance.

returns.doc: closest point information on success. null on failure.

methods.name: CollapseFacesByArea
methods.signature: public int CollapseFacesByArea(double lessThanArea, double greaterThanArea)
methods.summary: Collapses multiple mesh faces, with areas less than LessThanArea and greater than GreaterThanArea, based on the principles found in Stan Melax's mesh reduction PDF, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf
methods.remarks: This number may differ from the initial number of faces that meet the input criteria because the areas of some initial faces may be altered as other faces are collapsed. The face area must be both less than LessThanArea AND greater than GreaterThanArea in order to be considered. Use large numbers for lessThanArea or zero for greaterThanArea to simulate an OR.

params.name: lessThanArea
params.doc: Area in which faces are selected if their area is less than or equal to.

params.name: greaterThanArea
params.doc: Area in which faces are selected if their area is greater than or equal to.

returns.doc: Number of faces that were collapsed in the process.

methods.name: CollapseFacesByByAspectRatio
methods.signature: public int CollapseFacesByByAspectRatio(double aspectRatio)
methods.summary: Collapses a multiple mesh faces, determined by face aspect ratio, based on criteria found in Stan Melax's polygon reduction, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf
methods.remarks: This number may differ from the initial number of faces that meet the input criteria because the aspect ratios of some initial faces may be altered as other faces are collapsed.

params.name: aspectRatio
params.doc: Faces with an aspect ratio less than aspectRatio are considered as candidates.

returns.doc: Number of faces that were collapsed in the process.

methods.name: CollapseFacesByEdgeLength
methods.signature: public int CollapseFacesByEdgeLength(bool bGreaterThan, double edgeLength)
methods.summary: Collapses multiple mesh faces, with greater/less than edge length, based on the principles found in Stan Melax's mesh reduction PDF, see http://pomax.nihongoresources.com/downloads/PolygonReduction.pdf
methods.remarks: This number may differ from the initial number of edges that meet the input criteria because the lengths of some initial edges may be altered as other edges are collapsed.

params.name: bGreaterThan
params.doc: Determines whether edge with lengths greater than or less than edgeLength are collapsed.

params.name: edgeLength
params.doc: Length with which to compare to edge lengths.

returns.doc: Number of edges (faces) that were collapsed, -1 for general failure (like bad topology or index out of range) or -2 if all of the edges would be collapsed and the resulting mesh would be invalid.

methods.name: ColorAt(Int32, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public Color ColorAt(int faceIndex, double t0, double t1, double t2, double t3)
methods.summary: Evaluate a mesh normal at a set of barycentric coordinates. Barycentric coordinates must be assigned in accordance with the rules as defined by MeshPoint.T.
methods.remarks: Coordinate 0,0,0,0 is not a valid set of barycentric coordinates. The sum of t0 to t3 should be 1.

params.name: faceIndex
params.doc: Index of triangle or quad to evaluate.

params.name: t0
params.doc: First barycentric coordinate.

params.name: t1
params.doc: Second barycentric coordinate.

params.name: t2
params.doc: Third barycentric coordinate.

params.name: t3
params.doc: Fourth barycentric coordinate. If the face is a triangle, this coordinate will be ignored.

returns.doc: The interpolated vertex color on the mesh or Color.Transparent if the faceIndex is not valid, if the barycentric coordinates could not be evaluated, or if there are no colors defined on the mesh.

methods.name: ComputeCurvatureApproximation
methods.signature: public bool ComputeCurvatureApproximation(int type, out double[] perVertexCurvatures)
methods.summary: Compute an approximation of the discrete curvatures of the mesh vertices, according to which type of curvature information is requested. This method will not yield meaningful values on nonmanifold vertices, and nan on naked vertices. For now, this method operates solely on the mesh topology (the fully welded mesh), but distribtes the result across all the ordinary vertices. This method is a const and thread-safe method and will leave the m_K array untouched. An integer indicating which curvature is desired. gaussian_curvature = 1, mean_curvature = 2, minimum unsigned radius of curvature = 3, maximum unsigned radius of curvature = 4 Resulting curvature array on success, null on failure. On success, the length of the array is the number of vertices.

methods.name: CreatePatch
methods.signature: public static Mesh CreatePatch(Polyline outerBoundary, double angleToleranceRadians, Surface pullbackSurface, IEnumerable<Curve> innerBoundaryCurves, IEnumerable<Curve> innerBothSideCurves, IEnumerable<Point3d> innerPoints, bool trimback, int divisions)
methods.summary: Construct a mesh patch from a variety of input geometry.

params.name: outerBoundary
params.doc: (optional: can be null) Outer boundary polyline, if provided this will become the outer boundary of the resulting mesh. Any of the input that is completely outside the outer boundary will be ignored and have no impact on the result. If any of the input intersects the outer boundary the result will be unpredictable and is likely to not include the entire outer boundary.

params.name: angleToleranceRadians
params.doc: Maximum angle between unit tangents and adjacent vertices. Used to divide curve inputs that cannot otherwise be represented as a polyline.

params.name: pullbackSurface
params.doc: (optional: can be null) Initial surface where 3d input will be pulled to make a 2d representation used by the function that generates the mesh. Providing a pullbackSurface can be helpful when it is similar in shape to the pattern of the input, the pulled 2d points will be a better representation of the 3d points. If all of the input is more or less coplanar to start with, providing pullbackSurface has no real benefit.

params.name: innerBoundaryCurves
params.doc: (optional: can be null) Polylines to create holes in the output mesh. If innerBoundaryCurves are the only input then the result may be null if trimback is set to false (see comments for trimback) because the resulting mesh could be invalid (all faces created contained vertices from the perimeter boundary).

params.name: innerBothSideCurves
params.doc: (optional: can be null) These polylines will create faces on both sides of the edge. If there are only input points(innerPoints) there is no way to guarantee a triangulation that will create an edge between two particular points. Adding a line, or polyline, to innerBothsideCurves that includes points from innerPoints will help guide the triangulation.

params.name: innerPoints
params.doc: (optional: can be null) Points to be used to generate the mesh. If outerBoundary is not null, points outside of that boundary after it has been pulled to pullbackSurface (or the best plane through the input if pullbackSurface is null) will be ignored.

params.name: trimback
params.doc: Only used when a outerBoundary has not been provided. When that is the case, the function uses the perimeter of the surface as the outer boundary instead. If true, any face of the resulting triangulated mesh that contains a vertex of the perimeter boundary will be removed.

params.name: divisions
params.doc: Only used when a outerBoundary has not been provided. When that is the case, division becomes the number of divisions each side of the surface's perimeter will be divided into to create an outer boundary to work with.

returns.doc: mesh on success; null on failure

methods.name: CreateQuadSphere
methods.signature: public static Mesh CreateQuadSphere(Sphere sphere, int subdivisions)
methods.summary: Constructs a quad mesh sphere. A quad mesh sphere differs from a standard UV mesh sphere in that it's vertices are evenly distributed. A quad mesh sphere starts from a cube (a regular polyhedron with 6 square sides). It is then refined by splitting each quad into 4 smaller quads. This splitting can be done several times.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: The input sphere provides the orienting plane and radius.

params.name: subdivisions
params.doc: The number of times you want the faces split, where 0 <= subdivisions <= 8. Note, the total number of mesh faces produces is: 6 * (4 ^ subdivisions)

returns.doc: A welded quad mesh sphere if successful, or null on failure.

methods.summary: Attempts to "heal" naked edges in a mesh based on a given distance. First attempts to move vertices to neighboring vertices that are within that distance away. Then it finds edges that have a closest point to the vertex within the distance and splits the edge. When it finds one it splits the edge and makes two new edges using that point.

returns.doc: true if successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: IsPointInside
methods.signature: public bool IsPointInside(Point3d point, double tolerance, bool strictlyIn)
methods.summary: Determines if a point is inside a solid mesh.
methods.remarks: The caller is responsible for making certain the mesh is valid, closed, and 2-manifold before calling this function. If the mesh is not, the behavior is undetermined.

params.name: point
params.doc: 3d point to test.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: (>=0) 3d distance tolerance used for ray-mesh intersection and determining strict inclusion. This is expected to be a tiny value.

params.name: strictlyIn
params.doc: If strictlyIn is true, then point must be inside mesh by at least tolerance in order for this function to return true. If strictlyIn is false, then this function will return true if point is inside or the distance from point to a mesh face is <= tolerance.

returns.doc: true if point is inside the solid mesh, false if not.

methods.name: MatchEdges
methods.signature: public bool MatchEdges(double distance, bool rachet)
methods.summary: Moves face edges of an open mesh to meet adjacent face edges. The method will first try to match vertices, and then then it will try to split edges to make the edges match.

params.name: distance
params.doc: The distance tolerance. Use larger tolerances only if you select specific edges to close.

params.name: rachet
params.doc: If true, matching the mesh takes place in four passes starting at a tolerance that is smaller than your specified tolerance and working up to the specified tolerance with successive passes. This matches small edges first and works up to larger edges. If false, then a single pass is made.

returns.doc: True of edges were matched, false otherwise.

methods.name: SetCachedTextureCoordinatesFromMaterial
methods.signature: public void SetCachedTextureCoordinatesFromMaterial(RhinoObject rhinoObject, Material material)
methods.summary: Sets up cached texture coordinate set for each texture in the material. Textures in the Material define which mapping channels are used and the RhinoObject defines what texture mapping is used for each mapping channel. After this method is called all necessary texture coordinate sets are cached and correct texture coordinates for each texture can be fetched using GetCachedTextureCoordinates(RhinoObject rhinoObject, Rhino.DocObjects.Texture texture) If any texture coordinates are already cached they will not be re-computed.

params.name: rhinoObject
params.doc: RhinoObject that defines texture mappings

params.name: material
params.doc: Material with textures that define mapping channels

methods.name: SetSurfaceParametersFromTextureCoordinates
methods.signature: public bool SetSurfaceParametersFromTextureCoordinates()
methods.summary: If the mesh does not have surface evaluation parameters, has texture coordinates, and the surface parameters can be set in a way so the existing texture coordinates can be computed from the surface parameters, then this function sets the surface parameters. This is useful when meshes that have texture coordinates and do not have surface parameters want to set the surface parameters in a way so that the texture mapping of type TextureMappingType.SurfaceParameters will restore the texture coordinates.

returns.doc: true - successful false - failure - no changes made to the mesh

methods.summary: Split a mesh with a collection of meshes. Suggestion: upgrade to overload with tolerance. Does not split at coplanar intersections.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: Meshes to split with.

returns.doc: An array of mesh segments representing the split result.

methods.name: Split(Mesh)
methods.signature: public Mesh[] Split(Mesh mesh)
methods.summary: Split a mesh with another mesh. Suggestion: upgrade to overload with tolerance.

params.name: mesh
params.doc: Mesh to split with.

returns.doc: An array of mesh segments representing the split result.

methods.name: Split(Plane)
methods.signature: public Mesh[] Split(Plane plane)
methods.summary: Split a mesh by an infinite plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: The splitting plane.

returns.doc: A new mesh array with the split result. This can be null if no result was found.

methods.name: Split(IEnumerable<Mesh>, Double, Boolean, TextLog, CancellationToken, IProgress<Double>)
methods.signature: public Mesh[] Split(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, bool splitAtCoplanar, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)
methods.summary: Split a mesh with a collection of meshes.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: Meshes to split with.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A value for intersection tolerance. WARNING! Correct values are typically in the (10e-8 - 10e-4) range.An option is to use the document tolerance diminished by a few orders or magnitude.

params.name: splitAtCoplanar
params.doc: If false, coplanar areas will not be separated.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log to write onto.

params.name: cancel
params.doc: A cancellation token.

params.name: progress
params.doc: A progress reporter item. This can be null.

returns.doc: An array of mesh parts representing the split result, or null: when no mesh intersected, or if a cancel stopped the computation.

methods.name: Split(IEnumerable<Mesh>, Double, Boolean, Boolean, TextLog, CancellationToken, IProgress<Double>)
methods.signature: public Mesh[] Split(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, bool splitAtCoplanar, bool createNgons, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)
methods.summary: Split a mesh with a collection of meshes.

params.name: meshes

params.name: createNgons
params.doc: If true, creates ngons along the split ridge.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log to write onto.

params.name: cancel
params.doc: A cancellation token.

params.name: progress

returns.doc: An array of mesh parts representing the split result, or null: when no mesh intersected, or if a cancel stopped the computation.

methods.name: SplitDisjointPieces
methods.signature: public Mesh[] SplitDisjointPieces()
methods.summary: Splits up the mesh into its unconnected pieces.

returns.doc: An array containing all the disjoint pieces that make up this Mesh.

methods.name: SplitMesh
methods.signature: public static Mesh[] SplitMesh(Mesh mesh, int maxCount, bool countSum, bool countTriangles)
methods.summary: Some file formats and rendering engines are only able to process small batches of triangles. Use this function to split the mesh into subsets of vertices and faces that those file formats and rendering engines can handle.

params.name: mesh
params.doc: Mesh to split.

params.name: maxCount
params.doc: Maximum count.

params.name: countSum
params.doc: If true, split if total vertices + faces is greater than maxCount. If false, split if either total vertices or faces is greater than maxCount.

params.name: countTriangles
params.doc: If true, count triangles. If false, count triangles and quads.

returns.doc: An array of sub-meshes if successful, otherwise an empty array.

methods.name: SplitWithProjectedPolylines(IEnumerable<PolylineCurve>, Double)
methods.signature: public Mesh[] SplitWithProjectedPolylines(IEnumerable<PolylineCurve> curves, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Splits a mesh by adding edges in correspondence with input polylines, and divides the mesh at partitioned areas. Polyline segments that are measured not to be on the mesh will be ignored.

params.name: curves
params.doc: An array, a list or any enumerable of polyline curves.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value.

returns.doc: An array of meshes, or null if no change would happen.

methods.name: SplitWithProjectedPolylines(IEnumerable<PolylineCurve>, Double, TextLog, CancellationToken, IProgress<Double>)

methods.summary: Splits a mesh by adding edges in correspondence with input polylines, and divides the mesh at partitioned areas. Polyline segments that are measured not to be on the mesh will be ignored.

params.name: curves
params.doc: An array, a list or any enumerable of polyline curves.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log, or null.

params.name: cancel
params.doc: A cancellation token to stop the computation at a given point.

params.name: progress
params.doc: A progress reporter to inform the user about progress. The reported value is indicative.

returns.doc: An array of meshes, or null if no change would happen.

methods.name: UnifyNormals()
methods.signature: public int UnifyNormals()
methods.summary: Attempts to fix inconsistencies in the directions of mesh faces in a mesh. This function does not modify mesh vertex normals, it rearranges the mesh face winding and face normals to make them all consistent. Note, you may want to call Mesh.Normals.ComputeNormals() to recompute vertex normals after calling this functions.

returns.doc: number of faces that were modified.

methods.name: UnifyNormals(Boolean)
methods.signature: public int UnifyNormals(bool countOnly)
methods.summary: Attempts to fix inconsistencies in the directions of mesh faces in a mesh. This function does not modify mesh vertex normals, it rearranges the mesh face winding and face normals to make them all consistent. Note, you may want to call Mesh.Normals.ComputeNormals() to recompute vertex normals after calling this functions.

params.name: countOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the number of faces that would be modified is determined.

returns.doc: If countOnly=false, the number of faces that were modified. If countOnly=true, the number of faces that would be modified.

methods.name: Unweld
methods.signature: public void Unweld(double angleToleranceRadians, bool modifyNormals)
methods.summary: Makes sure that faces sharing an edge and having a difference of normal greater than or equal to angleToleranceRadians have unique vertices along that edge, adding vertices if necessary.

params.name: angleToleranceRadians
params.doc: Angle at which to make unique vertices.

params.name: modifyNormals
params.doc: Determines whether new vertex normals will have the same vertex normal as the original (false) or vertex normals made from the corresponding face normals (true)

methods.name: UnweldEdge
methods.signature: public bool UnweldEdge(IEnumerable<int> edgeIndices, bool modifyNormals)
methods.summary: Adds creases to a smooth mesh by creating coincident vertices along selected edges.

params.name: edgeIndices
params.doc: An array of mesh topology edge indices.

params.name: modifyNormals
params.doc: If true, the vertex normals on each side of the edge take the same value as the face to which they belong, giving the mesh a hard edge look. If false, each of the vertex normals on either side of the edge is assigned the same value as the original normal that the pair is replacing, keeping a smooth look.

returns.doc: true if successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: UnweldVertices
methods.signature: public bool UnweldVertices(IEnumerable<int> topologyVertexIndices, bool modifyNormals)
methods.summary: Ensures that faces sharing a common topological vertex have unique indices into the MeshVertexList collection.

params.name: topologyVertexIndices
params.doc: Topological vertex indices, from the MeshTopologyVertexList collection, to be unwelded. Use TopologyVertexIndex(Int32) to convert from vertex indices to topological vertex indices.

params.name: modifyNormals
params.doc: If true, the new vertex normals will be calculated from the face normal.

returns.doc: true if successful, false otherwise.

methods.signature: public void Weld(double angleToleranceRadians)
methods.summary: Makes sure that faces sharing an edge and having a difference of normal less than or equal to angleToleranceRadians share vertices along that edge, vertex normals are averaged.

params.name: angleToleranceRadians
params.doc: Angle at which to weld vertices.

methods.name: WithEdgeSoftening
methods.signature: public Mesh WithEdgeSoftening(double softeningRadius, bool chamfer, bool faceted, bool force, double angleThreshold)
methods.summary: Constructs new mesh from the current one, with edge softening applied to it.

params.name: softeningRadius
params.doc: The softening radius.

params.name: chamfer
params.doc: Specifies whether to chamfer the edges.

params.name: faceted
params.doc: Specifies whether the edges are faceted.

params.name: force
params.doc: Specifies whether to soften edges despite too large a radius.

params.name: angleThreshold
params.doc: Threshold angle (in degrees) which controls whether an edge is softened or not. The angle refers to the angles between the adjacent faces of an edge.

returns.doc: A new mesh with soft edges.

throws.when: If displacement failed because of an error. The exception message specifies the error.

params.name: tolerance

properties.name: ComponentStates
properties.summary: Gets access to the vertex hidden/visibility collection in this mesh. This is a runtime property and it is not saved in the 3dm file.

properties.name: DisjointMeshCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of disjoint (topologically unconnected) pieces in this mesh.

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: FaceNormals
properties.summary: Gets access to the face normal collection in this mesh.

properties.name: Faces
properties.summary: Gets access to the mesh face list.

properties.name: HasBrepForm
properties.summary: Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object

properties.name: HasCachedTextureCoordinates
properties.summary: Will return true if SetCachedTextureCoordinates has been called; otherwise will return false.

properties.name: HasPrincipalCurvatures
properties.summary: HasPrincipalCurvatures

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: IsClosed
properties.summary: Returns true if every mesh "edge" has two or more faces.

properties.name: IsDeformable
properties.summary: true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsOriented
properties.summary: Returns true if the mesh is manifold and every pair of faces that share an "edge" have compatible orientations.

properties.name: IsShallowDuplicate
properties.summary: Is this instance a 'shallow copy'. Shallow copies are no different than regular instances with the exception that they will convert to complete independent 'deep' copies if a non const operation is perform on the instance (something that mutates the internal data)

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Returns true if the mesh is solid. A "solid" is a closed oriented manifold.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: Ngons
properties.summary: Gets access to the mesh ngon list. Ngons represent groups of Faces (triangles + quads).Faces are used to tessellate an Ngon internally.When a triangle or quad is referenced in the Ngon list, it is no longer visualized and conceived as a single entity, but takes part of the Ngon.If you need to get access to both Ngons and the faces that are not referenced by Ngons, that is, all polygons that are visible in the mesh, then use the GetNgonAndFacesEnumerable() helper method.

properties.name: Normals
properties.summary: Gets access to the vertex normal collection in this mesh.

properties.name: ObjectType
properties.summary: Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.

properties.name: PartitionCount
properties.summary: Number of partition information chunks stored on this mesh based on the last call to CreatePartitions

properties.name: TextureCoordinates
properties.summary: Gets access to the vertex texture coordinate collection in this mesh.

properties.name: TopologyEdges
properties.summary: Gets the MeshTopologyEdgeList object associated with this mesh. This object stores edge connectivity.

properties.name: TopologyVertices
properties.summary: Gets the MeshTopologyVertexList object associated with this mesh. This object stores vertex connectivity and the indices of vertices that were unified while computing the edge topology.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use shareable set of information.

properties.name: UserStringCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of user strings.

properties.name: VertexColors
properties.summary: Gets access to the (optional) vertex color collection in this mesh.

properties.name: Vertices
properties.summary: Gets access to the vertices set of this mesh.
